project_structure: Single-panel Cocos Creator editor extension. All UI code lives in panel/index.js as an Editor.Panel.extend() call containing inline CSS (style property, lines 59-720) and HTML template (template property, lines 723-1360). The panel is declared as type 'dockable' in package.json with width:450, height:120, min-height:60. No external CSS files or component framework - everything is a single monolithic file.

relevant_files.rationale: Contains ALL layout CSS and HTML template inline. Lines 59-720 define the style property with every CSS rule causing oversized layout. Lines 723-1360 define the HTML template structure. This is the sole file that needs modification for the UI layout refactor.

Declares panel dimensions: width:450, height:120, min-height:60, type:'dockable'. These are the Cocos Creator panel constraints that define the target viewport the CSS must work within. The width:450 and min-height:60 confirm the narrow landscape form factor.

patterns: The CSS uses a design-token approach with CSS custom properties (--spacing-*, --radius-*, --bg-*, etc.) defined on :host. All sizing uses these tokens. The UI uses Cocos Creator's built-in <ui-button> web component. Styles are applied with heavy !important usage to override the editor's default ui-button styling. The layout uses flexbox for the main container (column direction) and rows, and CSS grid for search results. There are no responsive breakpoints for width < 600px in the main panel CSS (only in the settings modal). The 44px touch-target minimum is applied universally - appropriate for mobile but oversized for a desktop editor panel.
dependencies: Cocos Creator Editor framework providing Editor.Panel.extend() API and <ui-button> web component. The i18n-helper module for translations. The data-manager and logger modules for state. SVG icons are defined inline as path data in ICON_PATHS object (lines 12-27) and rendered via svgIcon() helper function (lines 38-55).
integration_points: The panel integrates with Cocos Creator's dockable panel system. The <ui-button> elements trigger Editor.Ipc.sendToMain() calls. The #coms container is dynamically populated with ui-button elements representing current node components. The #list-items container is dynamically populated with search results. Settings modal is toggled via JS. All event binding happens in the ready() lifecycle method.
constraints: 1) Panel viewport is ~300-450px wide, min-height 60px (package.json declares 120px default height). 2) Must use Cocos Creator's <ui-button> web component - cannot replace with standard HTML buttons. 3) All CSS must be inline in the style property string. 4) The editor's Shadow DOM encapsulation means :host styles are the root. 5) Must maintain all existing functionality (search, add, refresh, settings, component list). 6) Cannot add external CSS files or frameworks.

clarification_needs.question: Should the action buttons (add, refresh, settings) become icon-only square buttons, or retain any text/label?
clarification_needs.context: Currently the buttons contain only SVG icons but are styled as 44x44px purple blocks with full background fill. They could be reduced to ~24x24px icon-only buttons with transparent/subtle background, or kept as small filled buttons around 28x28px.
options: Icon-only with transparent background, ~24px (like VS Code toolbar buttons)
Small filled icon buttons, ~28px with subtle background
Keep current icon-in-button style but reduce to 32px

clarification_needs.question: Should the empty state / loading state areas be collapsed to minimal height or removed entirely when not showing results?
clarification_needs.context: The .list-state class sets min-height:140px and padding:24px for empty/loading states. In a panel that may be only 120px tall total, this alone exceeds the available space. Options range from keeping a small indicator to hiding it entirely.
options: Collapse to single-line text indicator (~24px height)
Show only a small icon + text in a compact row (~32px)
Remove empty state entirely, just show empty list area

_metadata.task_description: The previous UI renovation produced oversized buttons, wasted space, and poor layout for a narrow landscape editor panel. Need to identify ALL layout-related CSS/HTML issues and propose a compact, editor-tool-appropriate layout.

oversized_buttons.summary: Action buttons (add, refresh, settings) render as 44x44px purple (#6366f1) filled blocks instead of compact icon buttons

css_rules.problem: 44px height + 44px min-width + purple fill + 8px radius creates large prominent blocks. In a 120px-tall panel, a single row of 44px buttons + 44px input + padding already consumes ~76px (63% of panel height).
css_rules.fix: Reduce to 24-28px height, remove background fill, use transparent/hover-only background, reduce border-radius to 4px

excessive_spacing.summary: Container padding and gaps consume ~40px of vertical space before any content

css_rules.problem: 16px padding top+bottom = 32px. 12px gap between 4 rows = 36px. Total spacing overhead: ~68px in a 120px panel.
css_rules.fix: Reduce padding to 4-6px, reduce gap to 4px

css_rules.problem: 8px gap between buttons in a row is reasonable but could be tighter

wasted_empty_state.summary: Empty/loading states have min-height:140px + 24px padding, exceeding the entire panel height

css_rules.problem: 140px min-height alone exceeds the 120px default panel height. With 24px padding on each side, the empty state demands ~188px minimum.
css_rules.fix: Remove min-height entirely or set to 24px. Reduce padding to 4px. Use single-line compact text.

oversized_search_input.summary: Search input is 44px tall - mobile touch-target size, not desktop editor size

css_rules.problem: 44px height is mobile-optimized. Desktop editor inputs are typically 22-26px.
css_rules.fix: Reduce to 24px height, reduce padding to 0 6px, reduce font-size to 12px, reduce border-radius to 3px

oversized_component_buttons.summary: Component buttons in #coms area use 44px min-height with generous padding

css_rules.problem: 44px component buttons in a scrollable strip consume too much vertical space.
css_rules.fix: Reduce min-height to 22-24px, reduce padding to 2px 6px, reduce font-size to 11px

css_rules.problem: 120px max-height equals the entire panel default height. 8px padding and 8px gap are excessive.
css_rules.fix: Reduce max-height to 32-40px, reduce padding to 2px, reduce gap to 4px, reduce border-radius to 3px

search_results_oversized.summary: Search result items use 44px min-height and grid min-width 220px preventing compact layout

css_rules.problem: Each result takes 44px+ height. Only 1-2 results visible in 120px panel.
css_rules.fix: Reduce min-height to 24px, padding to 2px 6px, font-size to 12px, border-radius to 3px

css_rules.problem: 220px min-width means at 300px panel width, only 1 column is possible. At 450px, still only 1-2 columns.
css_rules.fix: Reduce minmax to 140px or switch to flex-wrap layout for compact items

results_meta_unnecessary_space.summary: The results-meta row adds unnecessary vertical space between search input and results

css_rules.problem: Separate row for result count adds 24px+ vertical space.
css_rules.fix: Integrate result count into the search input row or make it an overlay/inline element

design_token_values_too_generous.summary: The base CSS custom properties define spacing values suited for a standalone app, not a compact editor panel

css_rules.problem: These spacing tokens are designed for a web app, not a compact editor tool. Even the 'xs' value of 4px is generous for a tight panel.
css_rules.fix: Halve all spacing tokens: xs=2px, sm=4px, md=6px, lg=8px, xl=12px. Reduce radii: sm=2px, md=3px, lg=4px, xl=6px. Reduce font-size-body to 11-12px.

html_template_issues.summary: HTML structure has unnecessary wrapper nesting and separate rows that waste space

elements.problem: Standalone row between search and list just for result count
elements.fix: Move result count into search row or as absolute-positioned overlay

elements.problem: Contains large icon + text block for empty state
elements.fix: Replace with minimal single-line text, remove the large search icon

elements.problem: 6 skeleton cards for loading state - way too many for compact panel
elements.fix: Reduce to 2-3 skeleton cards with smaller height

elements.problem: search-row + results-meta + list + coms-row = 4 flex children with gaps between each
elements.fix: Merge results-meta into search-row. Consider making coms-row conditional/collapsible.

current_consumption.available_for_list: NEGATIVE - no space left for search results

proposed_consumption.available_for_list: 54px at 120px panel height - fits 2 compact results
